name: Growth of Nominal GDP and Relative New Purchasing Power

book_quotes.quote: Figures 15.3 and 15.4 show that there is indeed a strong correlation between the nominal GDP growth and new purchasing power (1951-1952 is the Korean War), and table 15.1 confirms a robust econometric relation between the two with dummy variables for the Korean War, the Volcker Shock
Figure 15.3 Growth of Nominal GDP and Relative New Purchasing Power, 1950-2010

GDP = Gross Domestic Product, Nominal, BEA, NIPA Table 1.15 (http://www.bea.gov/) pgdp = Gross Domestic Product Deflator Index, 2005 = 100, Seasonally Adjusted, BEA, NIPA Table 1.1.4, (http://www.bea.gov/)

CR = Total Domestic Credit = Monetary Authority Claims on Central or General Government + (Other Deposit Corp Claims on Central or General Government - Central or General Government Deposits in Other Deposit Corps) + Other Deposit Corp Claims on State and Local Government or Official Entities + Other Deposit Corp Claims on Private Sector = IMF, IFS Financial 1948-2011, lines 31 + (78 - 88) + 79 + 81 CA = BEA Balance on Current Account, Table 4.1. Foreign Transactions in the National Income and Product Accounts, line 29. PP = New purchasing power = CR + CA pp = Relative new purchasing power = PP/GDP

primary_source.publication: BEA NIPA Tables 1.1.5 (Nominal GDP), 1.1.4 (GDP Deflator), 4.1 (Foreign Transactions); IMF International Financial Statistics monetary survey lines 31, 78, 88, 79, 81
primary_source.table_or_series_id: BEA NIPA T1.1.5 line 1 (GDP), T1.1.4 line 1 (pGDP), T4.1 line 29 (CA); IMF IFS Financial lines 31+(78-88)+79+81 (CR)
primary_source.units: Rate (decimal) for gGDP and pp = PP/GDP
primary_source.license: BEA public domain; IMF IFS subscription/limited public access
primary_source.replaced_by: IMF IFS monetary survey line numbers reorganized post-2009 SDDS+ migration

formula: gGDP_t = ln(GDP_t/GDP_{t-1}); PP_t = CR_t + CA_t (Total Domestic Credit + Current Account Balance); pp_t = PP_t / GDP_t

components.label: Nominal GDP growth (gGDP)
components.source: BEA NIPA T1.1.5 line 1 first log-difference

extension_candidates.subseries_label: BEA NIPA + IMF IFS modern aggregates
extension_candidates.source: BEA NIPA Tables 1.1.5 / 4.1 + IMF IFS monetary survey
concerns: IMF IFS line-numbering changed post-2009; total Domestic Claims is now the more natural single-line aggregate.
Vintage revisions to NIPA GDP and current account can shift levels slightly.

extension_candidates.subseries_label: FRED Total Domestic Nonfinancial Debt (TCMDO replacement)
extension_candidates.source: Federal Reserve Z.1 Financial Accounts; FRED
concerns: Z.1 Domestic Nonfinancial Debt is a sectorally similar but not identical aggregate to IFS Total Domestic Credit.

methodology_notes: 'New purchasing power' is Shaikh's central inflation-theory variable: the sum of net new domestic credit creation and the current account balance, normalized by GDP.
Construction is multi-source — primary BEA NIPA for GDP and CA, IMF IFS for credit aggregates.
Figures 15.3 and 15.4 share underlying data; 15.4 is the scatter, 15.3 is the time-series.
CD2 predecessor S079 ported; CD2 cross-references S073-Q and S073-G as the upstream Appendix 15.2 series IDs.
open_questions: Phase 4: confirm IMF IFS line-number stability post-2009 and identify modern equivalent.
Phase 4: validate that BEA NIPA T1.15 reference in book (Appendix 15.1) is a typo for T1.1.5 (Nominal GDP) — book p.895 shows 'NIPA Table 1.15'; standard NIPA does not have a 1.15.

review_history.notes: CD2 S079 ported; quotes verified against PDF pp.703-704 and Appendix 15.1 pp.895-896

B7 BLOCKER RESOLUTION: IMF IFS line resolver implemented at Technical/code/loaders/_imf_ifs_resolver.py. Documentation at Technical/docs/methodology/IFS_line_to_SDMX_remap.md. The CH15 Phase 4 plan used the pre-2024 data.imf.org/IFS portal SRF codes (FOSAOP_XDC family); those codes were retired ca. 2024 when IMF migrated to the SDMX 3.0 REST API at api.imf.org. The resolver maps Shaikh's legacy lines through both eras: line 31 -> CBANK_NETAL_NCO_S1311MIXED (MFS_CBS); line 32 -> DCORP_N_DC (MFS_DC, canonical Net Domestic Claims); line 64 -> PCPI_IX (CPI); line 78 -> ODCORP_A_ACO_S1311MIXED (MFS_DC); line 79 -> ODCORP_A_ACO_S13M1 (MFS_DC); line 81 -> DCORP_A_ACO_PS (MFS_DC); line 88 -> ODCORP_L_LT_S1311MIXED (MFS_DC). Smoke test verified 2026-05-18: fetched DCORP_N_DC for USA 2018-2022 via api.imf.org SDMX 3.0 REST, HTTP 200, returned 5/5 years (2018=$21.16T, 2019=$22.42T, 2020=$25.76T, 2021=$30.12T, 2022=$29.91T). Phase 5 loader can now ingest this concept directly. Recommended Phase 5 fetch: DCORP_N_DC USA annual USD via api.imf.org; validate against Appendix15_USInflation.xlsx overlap 1948-2010 at +-2% tolerance using validate_against_shaikh().
